---
title: "Analysis Methods and Results"
subtitle: "Companion to 'Inventing Climate Finance' (submitted to *Oeconomia*)"
author: "Minh Ha-Duong"
date: "2026-04-14"
metadata-files: [technical-report-vars.yml]
bibliography: bibliography/main.bib
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
---

This document describes the analyses that transform the climate finance corpus (see the companion corpus report for construction and data quality) into the manuscript's figures and tables. It is a companion to the code repository: the document a reviewer or replicator reads alongside the scripts.

All scripts are in `scripts/`, generated data in `$CLIMATE_FINANCE_DATA/catalogs/`, and outputs in `content/figures/` and `content/tables/`.

# Corpus Summary

The analysis uses a multilingual corpus of {{< meta corpus_total >}} works on climate finance (1990--2024), assembled from {{< meta corpus_sources >}} bibliographic sources (see the corpus report for construction details). Of these, {{< meta corpus_core >}} constitute a "core" subset (cited $\geq$ {{< meta corpus_core_threshold >}} times). Sentence-transformer embeddings ({{< meta emb_dimensions >}} dimensions, `BAAI/bge-m3`) are available for {{< meta corpus_with_embeddings >}} works.

\newpage

# Structural Break Detection

{{< include _includes/structural-breaks.md >}}

---

# Thematic Structure

{{< include _includes/alluvial-diagram.md >}}

---

{{< include _includes/clustering-comparison.md >}}

---

<!-- WARNING: AI-generated include, not human-reviewed -->
{{< include _includes/temporal-structure.md >}}

\newpage

# Polarization Analysis

{{< include _includes/bimodality-analysis.md >}}

---

{{< include _includes/pca-scatter.md >}}

\newpage

# Citation Analysis

{{< include _includes/citation-genealogy.md >}}

---

{{< include _includes/cocitation-communities.md >}}
